channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Raised tree roots causing trip hazard, 25th June
item.description:
There is a tree and lots of roots protruding in the pavement right outside a black gate on the left before the bus stop.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 133, Saint Georges Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: The Public Footpath (EBW2) is illegally blockked, 25th June
item.description:
The footpath (PROW) is being blocked. There is no access to the path walking north from St. George's churchyard. I have not checked the northern boundary of Brockworth Court, but I suspect that a similar illegal closure has been put in place at that point.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 25th June
item.description:
Overgrown tree causing obstruction.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees implicated in subsidence of adjacent building, 25th June
item.description:
Subsidence Damage at Nuffield Health Gloucester Fitness & Wellbeing Gym, Centre Seven, Barnwood, GL4 3HR (Land Registry tile No. GR327038 - Land at A40, Barnwood, Gloucester) We act on behalf of the property owners in relation to a claim for suspected subsidence damage affecting the above property, in conjunction with their insurers. Our initial investigations identified a group of trees, believed to be maintained by National Highways on land owned by Gloucester City Council, as the principal cause of, or a material contributor to, the suspected subsidence cracking at the property. This was confirmed in the attached report by MWA Arboriculture Ltd (ref. SUB250918-16673). In addition to the arborist’s report, we have enclosed a report from Curtins Consulting Ltd dated 23rd of October 2025 (ref. 080427-CUR), together with a further report and quotation from Catalyst dated 5 November 2025 (ref. 135520). Structural monitoring has now commenced and all trees and vegetation within the property boundary identified by the arborist for removal were addressed. In the first instance, please arrange for the TG2 group to be removed to create a clear minimum distance of 15 metres from the structure in-line with the arborist’s recommendations, please also confirm that the TG2 group will be maintained to prevent future overgrowth. We understand that the land in question forms a part of the adopted highways areas and, as such, comes under the jurisdiction of the Gloucestershire Highways Authority and any claims of this nature should be directed to you. Please also note that this letter can be produced as evidence in future that you have been notified of the damage being caused by this vegetation and advised to take appropriate action and abate the nuisance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A417, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 25th June
item.description:
Literally moments after the last lot of rubbish was removed this mattress arrived. CBC please do your duty and stop this continuous illegal behaviour. It’s a blight to the area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Pates Avenue, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 25th June
item.description:
The last load of rubbish has already been replaced by yet more rubbish. Why does the council do absolutely nothing to try and catch those people responsible for this continuous illegal behaviour?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Pates Avenue, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sticker on new street sign, 25th June
item.description:
I know this isnt important in the grand scheme of things but some idiot has put a sticker on the lovely new street sign you installed. Ive tried peeling it off but it wont budge. If the cleaning team has some sticky label remover and are passing by would be great if they could remove it before someone adds to it. Thanks in advance
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 61, Brookbank Close, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerously Uneven pavement, 25th June
item.description:
The tree roots have raised the pavement and caused it to be uneven. The problem is that my dad who is 91 cannot get past this tree root as it causes his mobility scooter to ground. He now cannot get to the local shop or post office

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Byron Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Overgrown long dry grass near shops at 102 Insley Gardens, 25th June
item.description:
Last cut many months ago, and now around 2 foot high - in all areas near to shops and all around Insley Gardens whole area
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gilpin Avenue, Hucclecote, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large areas of Queens Court road in need of repair, 25th June
item.description:
Hi I manage Queens Court on Queens Road (GL50 2LU), and I understand that the roadway falls under the responsibility of Cheltenham Borough Council. I am unsure whether this should be reported through your Street site or if it is something that needs to be assessed by your team initially. Large sections of the roadway are currently in poor condition and, given that many of our residents are elderly, this is becoming an increasing concern due to the potential risk of trips and falls. I would therefore be grateful if you could advise on the appropriate process and whether resurfacing works at Queens Court could be considered for inclusion in your schedule of works as a matter of urgency. I look forward to hearing from you. Many thanks Nick Budgen Ash & Co nickbudgen@ashproperty.co.uk

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38-49, Queens Court, Warden Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
